content.full_text:
March 12, 1992

Sandra Taylor  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA., 95804-0807

Dear Sandra,

Thank you for your assistance in answering my questions concerning a letter to be sent on my behalf as a candidate for the 3rd District Assembly Republican nomination.

The following is my understanding of your interpretation of FPPC ruling: the letter may have the appearance of a chain letter inasmuch as the recipient will be asked to make copies and send on to friends, the letter may ask for contributions of money and/or help, in the event we mail 200 or more like letters, we must have the name of the committee and office sought on the letter and envelope in 6 point type or greater, we must establish a method for tracking total inkind contributions of individuals sending a copied letter and it is permissible to send the letter by FAX as well as by mail.

Each letter will have a tear off portion at the bottom which may be noted the contribution and the number of copied letters sent.

Again, thank you for your time.

Most sincerely,

Mary Andrews  
Mayor of Chico  
Candidate for 3rd District Assembly  
Republican Nomination - 1992
